title: Pleopeltis albidosquamata
intro: Pleopeltis albidosquamata, also known as the white-scaled polypody, is a species of fern native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleopeltis albidosquamata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are tea infusions and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues and reduce inflammation.
uses: Pleopeltis albidosquamata is a species of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America.
